Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Production levels increased on site compared to the previous year.  Because of that, the amount of fuel to be consumed for the drying process went up.  Our fuel of choice would have been natural gas, however, the gas distribution company did not have enough capacity in the line in order to meet the increased demand.  

Because of this, we used propane gas for heating.  However, this is only temporary.  Once the gas distribution company can increase production, we will switch back to natural gas as a fuel for heating.

Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site
Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site
Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Overall, the 2020 operational year had lower emissions and production compared to the previous year. This was due to a decrease in market demand resulting from less oil and gas activity given COVID-19 and low commodity pricing. I also noticed a mistake in the 2019 NPRI compliance report from the Propane Heaters calculation workbook. The submission from last year did not properly convert L of propane into m3 for the input tab and ended up with much higher NO2 and CO emissions.

p2Plan.noActivitiesComment:
Baghouses were previously installed in 2018/2019.

Currently awaiting parts availability to install a regulator valve that would aim to reduce Natural Gas consumption and emission. Expecting to receive the valve in 2023.
